explanation: This requirement states that whenever 'prop_1' occurs, 'prop_0' must also occur at the same time. In LTL, this is represented by saying that it is always the case (G) that if 'prop_1' holds, then 'prop_0' holds as well.
The requirement states that whenever 'prop_1' occurs, 'prop_0' must also occur. In LTL, this is often formalized as: globally, if 'prop_1' holds, then at that moment 'prop_0' must also hold.

The requirement implies that whenever 'prop_1' occurs, 'prop_0' should occur as well. In LTL, this is best captured by stating that it is always the case that if 'prop_1' is true, then 'prop_0' is also true at the same moment.
The requirement specifies that whenever 'prop_1' holds, then 'prop_0' must also hold at that time. In LTL, this is expressed as: globally, if 'prop_1' is true, then 'prop_0' must also be true.
Whenever prop_1 becomes true, then eventually prop_0 should happen. This is captured by saying: globally, if prop_1 holds, then eventually prop_0 will hold.

The requirement states that whenever 'prop_1' holds, 'prop_0' must also hold at the same time. This is captured by stating that it should always be the case (globally, G) that if 'prop_1' is true, then 'prop_0' is also true.

The phrase 'The prop_0 when the prop_1' indicates that whenever prop_1 is true, prop_0 must hold at the same time. In LTL, this means globally, if prop_1 holds, then at that moment prop_0 must also hold.

This requirement specifies that whenever 'prop_1' is true, 'prop_0' should also be true at that time. In LTL, this expresses as globally (G), whenever 'prop_1' holds, 'prop_0' should hold: G (prop_1 -> prop_0).
This specifies that whenever 'prop_1' is true, 'prop_0' shall also be true. This can be captured by the LTL formula: globally, if 'prop_1' then 'prop_0'.

The phrase 'The prop_0 when the prop_1.' suggests that whenever prop_1 holds, then prop_0 must also hold at that time. In LTL, this kind of relationship is expressed using the 'globally' operator G with an implication: G (prop_1 -> prop_0). This means, at all times, if prop_1 is true, prop_0 must also be true.
